title: Suspected ADR Signal Extraction Output
description: Structured output a Claude extraction skill must return for a single clinical note. Layers are separated so that the scorer can evaluate drug-reaction pairs (primary), reaction concepts (secondary), causality assertions (secondary), and overcall behaviour on negative notes (safety).

note_id.description: Identifier copied verbatim from the input note.

suspected_adr_signals.description: PRIMARY layer. Each item is a drug-reaction pair the note links by evidence.

reaction_text.description: Reaction/event span as written in the note.

normalized_reaction.description: MedDRA Preferred Term if known, otherwise plain-English canonical term (e.g. 'acute kidney injury').

suspected_drug.description: Suspected medication as written in the note (generic preferred if stated).

evidence_quote.description: Exact contiguous substring of the note that supports this pair. Must appear verbatim in note_text.

reasoning_brief.description: One sentence justifying the link, using only information present in the note.

unlinked_adverse_events.description: SECONDARY layer. Adverse events present in the note that are NOT linked to a medication.

negative_controls.description: SAFETY layer. Spans that look ADR-like but must NOT be reported as active ADR signals.
